title: ipam resolve input
description: Class-level, PROVIDER-AGNOSTIC (ADR-0111) allocation request to an ipam provider's resolve Action: carve a prefix (and optionally a VLAN) from a pool or role, scoped by enterprise topology (region/AZ/tenant/VLAN group). One shape for every provider (NetBox, Nautobot, CAPI-IPAM), so a provider swap changes no consumer. `pool` and `role` are mutually exclusive (oneOf) — exactly one names the allocation source; no implicit precedence (§2.4). The scope fields reference the EXISTING ADR-0059 topology Entity kinds (region, availability-zone), never a new concept.

key.description: The STABLE request identity (e.g. the subnet's name). The provider anchors idempotency on it (a re-resolve returns the same allocation) — ADR-0111 D4/F1. Stratt holds no allocation record; the mapping lives in the provider (a NetBox prefix description).

pool.description: The parent prefix/supernet (or provider pool id) to carve from.

role.description: A named allocation role/purpose the provider maps to a pool.

size.description: The requested prefix length (e.g. 24 for a /24; IPv4/IPv6 determined by the pool).

region.description: Scope: the region Entity name (ADR-0059) to allocate within.

tenant.description: Scope: the sovereignty/ownership tenant to allocate within.

vlanGroup.description: Scope: the VLAN group to draw a VLAN id from (when a VLAN is requested).
